title: Document Phase 3: Wire config into scheduler, skills, and prompts

handoff_summary.completed:
- Added "Scheduler config wiring" subsection with full table of instance attributes, config sources, and defaults
- Added "Skills config wiring" subsection documenting prepare_isolated_execution_root config parameter and AGENT_SKILL_ALLOWLIST intentional non-externalization
- Added "Prompts config wiring" subsection documenting optional templates_dir and agent_types overrides
- Updated project layout descriptions for scheduler.py, prompts.py, skills.py to note config integration
- Updated Key Concepts section: followup beads reference config.scheduler.followup_suffixes, corrective beads reference config.scheduler.transient_block_patterns and max_corrective_attempts

execution_history.summary: Updated CLAUDE.md to document Phase 3 config wiring into scheduler, skills, and prompts. Added three new subsections (Scheduler config wiring, Skills config wiring, Prompts config wiring) detailing how each module reads from OrchestratorConfig instead of hardcoded constants. Updated project layout annotations and key concepts to reference config paths.

guardrails.template_text:
# Documentation Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Update documentation and examples that explain the assigned behavior without changing runtime feature behavior.

Allowed actions:
- Edit docs, examples, and explanatory text tied to the assigned bead.
- Align documentation with existing code and validated behavior.
- Identify when implementation or tests must land first and block with a handoff.

Disallowed actions:
- Change runtime behavior, production code paths, or feature logic.
- Invent undocumented behavior that is not present in the codebase.
- Approve code quality or test completeness as a substitute for review.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise documentation changes and remaining gaps.
- Accurate updated docs and clear next-agent recommendations when code changes are required first.
